chunk_summary: This section focuses on recognizing and managing catheter occlusion and thrombosis in enteral and parenteral nutrition, emphasizing monitoring thresholds and initial response strategies based on pediatric guidelines.
learning_objectives: Identify signs and symptoms of catheter occlusion and thrombosis in pediatric patients receiving parenteral nutrition.
Understand initial management strategies for catheter-related complications.
Apply evidence-based guidelines to prevent and address catheter occlusion and thrombosis.
Recognize the importance of monitoring thresholds in managing complications.
key_facts: Catheter occlusion is a common complication in parenteral nutrition, with thrombotic occlusion being a primary cause.
Initial management includes assessing catheter patency and using thrombolytic agents if indicated.
Pediatric guidelines recommend regular monitoring of catheter function to prevent complications.
Thrombosis risk increases with prolonged catheter use and improper maintenance protocols.
ESPEN guidelines emphasize the importance of early recognition and intervention to reduce morbidity.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: ESPGHAN/ESPEN/ESPR guidelines emphasize the importance of early recognition and management of catheter-related complications to minimize risks in pediatric parenteral nutrition.
